data.body:
# Overlay reviewer source map — ROUND 2 (API-compatibility, Dependencies/supply-chain, Database/migrations)

Round-2 additional overlays for the base+overlay architecture (epics 9da1-cb98-2e94-4f2b / b744-4fb9-2d05-4b49). VERDICT: all 3 strong, usable, PERMISSIVELY-licensed — NONE blocked. Common theme: no domain ships a drop-in LLM review prompt; each provides tool check-taxonomies that translate cleanly to a locked rubric (same scanner-as-ground-truth + LLM-judge meta-pattern as round 1).

## API compatibility / backwards-compat — STRONG
- Sources (all permissive): buf breaking (Apache-2.0; proto tiered WIRE/WIRE_JSON/PACKAGE/FILE), oasdiff (Apache-2.0; OpenAPI 450+ rules, ERR/WARN/INFO), graphql-js findSchemaChanges (MIT; Breaking/Dangerous/Non-breaking trichotomy), graphql-inspector (MIT; usage-aware), Griffe (ISC; Python BreakageKind enum), cargo-semver-checks (Apache/MIT; Rust), japicmp/revapi (Apache; Java source-vs-binary), @microsoft/api-extractor (MIT; TS release tags). Standards: SemVer (CC-BY), Conventional Commits (CC-BY/MIT), Google AIP-180/181/185 (Apache/CC-BY).
- Dimensions: (A) symbol existence (removal/rename=remove+add); (B) signature/shape (param removed/reordered/kind; NEW REQUIRED param = silent breaker; type change; cardinality/optionality); (C) return/output contract (narrowed return; removed response property/status code); (D) enum/closed-set (removed value=BREAKING; ADDED value=DANGEROUS); (E) wire/serialization tier (wire-safe-but-source-breaking = severity modifier; field-number reuse); (F) validation/semantics tightening (stricter validation; changed default=DANGEROUS; SAME-SIGNATURE-CHANGED-BEHAVIOR = LLM-only, NO tool catches it); (G) deprecation/versioning correctness (MAJOR bump taken? Conventional-Commits flagged? deprecation-before-removal ~90d? reserve-not-reuse).
- Severity vocab to adopt: BREAKING / DANGEROUS / NON_BREAKING / INFO (graphql-js) + ERR vs WARN (oasdiff). The DANGEROUS/WARN bucket routes to the LLM.
- Triggers: *.proto/buf.yaml; openapi*/swagger*; *.graphql/*.gql; version-field bumps; api-extractor *.api.md; *.pyi/*.d.ts/__all__ public surface. Semantic: exported-symbol diff (preferred, lower FP). Base-escalation critical for same-signature-changed-behavior, hand-rolled HTTP/RPC w/o schema artifact, config-as-API.
- LICENSE: all permissive, NO blockers. FLAGS: (a) no LLM-specific rubric exists -> author original; (b) some tools miss dims (Griffe RETURN_CHANGED_TYPE not-supported-yet; cargo-semver-checks misses signature/generics/lifetimes) -> those lean on LLM/escalation.

## Dependencies / supply-chain — STRONG
- Sources (Apache/MIT): OpenSSF Scorecard (19 checks = best taxonomy), Google OSV-Scanner, OWASP Dependency-Check, Trivy/Grype (EPSS+KEV severity), pip-audit, GitHub dependency-review-action (MIT; closest PR-time rubric); SLSA + SPDX for provenance/license.
- 7 dimensions: known vulns (CVE/OSV, weight CVSS+EPSS+KEV); malicious/typosquat/slopsquat (install scripts, obfuscation, behavioral); license compatibility (SPDX + copyleft intrusion); pinning/lockfile hygiene (hash pinning; manifest<->lockfile consistency); maintenance health (Scorecard Maintained/Contributors/Code-Review); minimal/justified additions (anti-bloat); integrity/provenance (signed releases, SBOM, SLSA).
- Triggers: all ecosystem manifest+lockfile globs (package.json/lock, requirements/poetry.lock/uv.lock, go.mod/sum, Cargo.toml/lock, Gemfile/lock, pom.xml/build.gradle, composer, *.csproj). Deterministic escalation: manifest changed WITHOUT matching lockfile change.
- LICENSE FLAGS: Snyk proprietary (concept-only); Socket product-docs (concepts not text; 403-snippet-sourced); ScanCode LicenseDB CC-BY-4.0 (attribution IF dataset embedded — avoidable via public-domain SPDX ids). NOT blocked. npm/yarn audit miss novel malware -> dim-2 must be a separate LLM-reasoned check.

## Database / migrations / SQL — STRONG
- Sources: strong_migrations (MIT; best unsafe-op->safe-rewrite), Atlas lint analyzers (Apache-2.0; 50+ DB-aware), django-migration-linter (Apache-2.0; reversibility), sqlfluff (MIT; STYLE ONLY not safety); CodeRabbit migration checks = behavior benchmark (up/down required, destructive-without-rollback, missing-migration).
- 7 dimensions: D1 destructive/irreversible (DROP; rename-as-destructive; lossy type change); D2 locking/zero-downtime (non-CONCURRENTLY index; NOT NULL+default rewrite; validating constraint scan -> NOT VALID then VALIDATE); D3 index correctness/query perf; D4 backfill/data-migration safety (batched not single-txn; fail-on-existing-rows); D5 reversibility (down-migrations; RunSQL/RunPython reverse); D6 transaction safety (CONCURRENTLY-in-txn illegal; lock/statement timeout); D7 backward-compat/expand-contract (reward expand->migrate->contract; flag single-step breaking). Optional D8 type hygiene (bigint/identity/timestamptz).
- Triggers: migrations/migrate/alembic/db-migrate/Flyway/Liquibase/prisma-migrations/drizzle/goose globs + *.sql (scope-gated) + schema files. High-value escalation: ORM model changed but NO migration file present.
- LICENSE FLAG: Squawk distributed npm/PyPI packages GPL-3.0 (Rust core MIT/Apache) -> use only as factual coverage cross-check; source wording from strong_migrations/Atlas/django-migration-linter (every Squawk rule has a permissive equivalent). pt-osc GPL-2.0 (rationale only). NO NC/SA blockers; NOT blocked.

## Round-2 flags summary
- NONE blocked. All 3 permissively sourced. Recurring: NO drop-in LLM prompt exists for any of these domains -> author the rubric from permissive tool taxonomies (facts, not copyrightable). Isolated copyleft/proprietary items each have a permissive alternative: deps Snyk/Socket(proprietary)->OSV/Scorecard/Grype; DB Squawk(GPL)->strong_migrations/Atlas; API-compat = all permissive.
- The DANGEROUS/WARN-class findings (added enum value, changed default, tightened validation, behavioral semantics) are the LLM-judgment zone routed through our pass-2 binary-verifier + pass-3 deterministic gate.

## Overlay research status: COMPLETE
Base = Google eng-practices (sourced). Overlays sourced: security (Anthropic, round 0); round 1 = infra, testing, documentation, performance, LLM-prompts/skills; round 2 = API-compat, dependencies/supply-chain, DB/migrations. All have popular, actively-maintained, usable, permissively-sourceable criteria. Next: consolidate into the per-overlay catalog entries (criteria source + license/attribution + deterministic trigger globs + base-escalation signal) for epics E2 (9da1) / E3 (b744).
